title: Australia, New Zealand, and Pacific: Analysis, Research, & Events | CSIS
gist: This is a CSIS topic-landing page for Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific; it is a directory of commentary, podcasts and events rather than a single analytical article, signaling CSIS's research focus on US-China strategic competition in the Indo-Pacific, AUKUS, and Pacific Islands engagement.

insights.claim: CSIS frames its Australia, New Zealand and Pacific work around advancing AUKUS, deepening economic and technological exchange with Australia, elevating cooperation with New Zealand, and bolstering US engagement in the Pacific Islands amid rising Indo-Pacific strategic competition.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Maps the institutional priorities shaping US Indo-Pacific alliance policy; relevant to defense-cooperation and critical-minerals themes.
insights.quote: advancing AUKUS, elevating cooperation with New Zealand, and bolstering U.S. engagement in the Pacific Islands amid rising strategic competition in the Indo-Pacific.

insights.claim: A CSIS commentary argues Matthew Wale's rise to power in the Solomon Islands offers Washington its best opportunity in years to rebuild ties, but seizing it will require more than diplomatic rhetoric.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Flags a contested Pacific state where US-China influence is in play; watch for shifts in Solomon Islands alignment.
insights.quote: Matthew Wale's rise to power offers Washington its best opportunity in years to rebuild ties with the Solomon Islands, but seizing the moment will require more than diplomatic rhetoric.

insights.claim: CSIS notes that Australia and Fiji's new Vuvale Union treaty redefines Pacific security as Canberra moves to counter China's growing regional influence.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Indicates intensifying Australia-led counter-China security architecture in the Pacific.
insights.quote: Australia and Fiji's new Vuvale Union further redefines Pacific security as Canberra moves to counter China's growing regional influence.
insights.locator: What Australia and Fiji's New Treaty Means for the Pacific
